text: Control of surface strain in catalysts by inducing structural changes in metallic cores is highly desirable but rarely realized in practical catalytic systems. Here, we show that the surface strain of Pt in a Pt shell core/shell FePt NP structure can be controlled while the same NP structure is preserved, where we obtain 3 Pt layers with different surface strains. Through QM-MM calculations, we confirmed that high ORR performance of fct-FePt NPs is caused by relieving the over-compression strain in the Pt surface layers, which was induced by the large lattice mismatch between the fcc FePt and Pt. By tuning the lattice mismatch between FePt and Pt shells by partially replacing Fe by Cu in fct-FeCuPt/Pt, we finally obtained an extremely high specific activity towards the ORR, which is about 10 times as large as that of commercial Pt catalysts. The present structure-induced surface strain control method will open new avenues to design high-performance catalysts for the ORR as well as other catalytic reactions.
